name: Republic of China (1912–1928)
description: Five horizontal stripes of equal width — red, yellow, blue, white, black — from top to bottom.
history: The 'Five-Coloured Flag' (五色旗) was the national flag of the Republic of China during the Beiyang government (1912–1928). The five colours symbolised the 'Five Races Under One Union' — the five major ethnic groups of China envisioned as forming the new republic. Replaced in 1928 by the current ROC flag (Blue Sky, White Sun, and a Wholly Red Earth) when the Kuomintang's Northern Expedition unified the country.

features.symbolism: the 'Five Races Under One Union' — Han, Manchu, Mongol, Hui, Tibetan
